grader_results.details: score=3.0/5 (min=3.0): Error handling is consistently implemented but exposes validation details and lacks structured logging for fintech requirements. | Findings: [high] Multiple routes (e.g., calibration/sessions/[id]/annotate/route.ts): Validation errors expose internal schema details through Zod error.issues; [medium] All error handlers: Error logging lacks structured context (request ID, user, tenant); [medium] src/app/api/contracts/check-drift/route.ts line 69-73: Silent catch block for JSON parsing could mask malformed requests; [low] All routes: Generic 'Internal server error' message provides no actionable information

score=3.0/5 (min=3.0): Components show moderate React patterns but have significant issues with unnecessary client directives, missing error handling, and accessibility gaps that need addressing for production fintech use. | Findings: [high] Multiple components: All components use 'use client' directive but many don't need client-side features. Components like yaml-preview.tsx, case-status-badge.tsx, tag-badge.tsx only render static UI without hooks or event handlers; [medium] annotation-form.tsx:buildPayload: Function creates inline objects on every call which could cause unnecessary re-renders when passed as props; [high] Multiple list components: Missing key props on list items in several components including field-input.tsx (line 134, 165), annotation-queue.tsx; [high] alert-panel.tsx, annotation-form.tsx: fetch() calls without proper error handling for non-200 responses and network failures; [medium] Multiple form components: Interactive buttons missing explicit type='button' attribute, which defaults to 'submit' in forms (+3 more)

score=3.0/5 (min=3.0): Code demonstrates good SQL injection protection and input validation, but lacks proper authorization controls and exposes internal implementation details that could aid attackers in a multi-tenant fintech environment. | Findings: [medium] Multiple API routes (contracts, skills, golden-sets): Internal database UUIDs exposed in API responses without obfuscation; [high] All API routes: No tenant isolation or user-based access controls - any authenticated user can access any record; [medium] /api/contracts/check-drift/route.ts:flattenKeys(): Full internal schema structure exposed in API responses; [medium] Contract and version endpoints: Inconsistent PII redaction - some endpoints redact sensitive data while others expose full schema snapshots
